title: manihot rhomboidea subsp. microcarpa
intro: Manihot rhomboidea subsp. microcarpa, also known as wild cassava or bitter cassava, is a plant species that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its rich content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Manihot rhomboidea subsp. microcarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Manihot rhomboidea subsp. microcarpa include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by boiling the roots or leaves of the plant in water.
